tools.name: crypto_marketdata-best-bid-ask
tools.description: Fetch a single bid and ask price per symbol, representing the best available price across our partner market makers. This price does not take into account the order size, and may not be the final execution price. The bid and ask prices include a spread. The buy spread is the percent difference between the ask and the mid price. The sell spread is the percent difference between the bid and the mid price. Note that in the v2 endpoint, **Place crypto orders with fee tiers**, partner exchanges provide prices instead of market makers, with orders routed accordingly. For more information on routing, visit our [ Help Center ](https://robinhood.com/us/en/support/articles/crypto-order-routing/). Optional: symbol. Returns the CryptoMarketdataBestBidAskResponse.

params.name: symbol
params.description: List of trading pair symbol(s) to retrieve data for. Multiple symbols can be provided by using the 'symbol' parameter with different values. Ensure that the symbol(s) are provided in all uppercase. For example, **?symbol=BTC-USD&symbol=ETH-USD**

tools.name: crypto_marketdata-best-bid-ask-marketdata
tools.description: Fetch a single bid and ask price per symbol, representing the best available price across our partner exchanges. This price does not take into account the order size or fee, and may not be the final execution price. Required: symbol. Returns the V2BestBidAskResponse.

params.name: symbol
params.description: List of trading pair symbol(s) to retrieve data for. Multiple symbols can be provided by using the 'symbol' parameter with different values. Ensure that the symbol(s) are provided in all uppercase. Only USD symbols that have `is_api_tradable=true` as defined in our Get Crypto Trading Pairs endpoint are accepted. For example, `?symbol=BTC-USD&symbol=ETH-USD`

tools.name: crypto_marketdata-estimated-price
tools.description: This endpoint returns the estimated total cost or credit for a particular symbol, book side, and asset quantity. You can include a list of quantities in a single request to retrieve the price for various hypothetical order sizes. The estimated price represents the expected execution price if you were to subsequently place an order. To estimate the cost for a Buy order, request an Ask quote. If you are preparing to place a Sell order, request a Bid quote. The execution price may vary due to market volatility and once executed the transaction may not be undone. The bid and ask prices are the best prices our market makers provide for an order, inclusive of a spread. The buy spread is the percent difference between the ask and the mid price. The sell spread is the percent difference between the bid and the mid price. Required: symbol, side, quantity.

params.name: symbol
params.description: A string matching a valid currency pair, for example BTC-USD. Ensure that the symbol is provided in all uppercase. Only USD symbols are accepted.

params.name: side
params.description: Order side. e.g. bid, ask, or both.

params.name: quantity
params.description: Quantity must be between `min_order_size` and `max_order_size` as defined in our `Get Crypto Trading Pairs` endpoint. A max of 10 quantities can be specified for each request.

tools.name: crypto_marketdata-estimated-price-trading
tools.description: This endpoint returns the estimated total cost or credit for a particular symbol, book side, asset quantity, and fee. You can include a list of quantities in a single request to retrieve the price for various hypothetical order sizes. The estimated price represents the expected execution price if you were to subsequently place an order. The execution price may vary due to market volatility and once executed the transaction may not be undone. The bid and ask prices are the best prices our partner exchanges provide for an order, excluding the fee. Required: symbol, side, quantity.

params.name: symbol
params.description: A string matching a valid currency pair, for example `BTC-USD`. Ensure that the symbol is provided in all uppercase. Only USD symbols that have `is_api_tradable=true` as defined in our Get Crypto Trading Pairs endpoint are accepted.

params.name: side
params.description: Order side: `bid`, `ask`, or `both`.

params.name: quantity
params.description: Quantity must be smaller than `max_order_size` as defined in our Get Crypto Trading Pairs endpoint. Multiple quantities can be comma-separated. A max of 10 quantities can be specified for each request.

tools.name: crypto_post-trading-cancel-order
tools.description: Cancels an open crypto trading order. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: Order ID, unique per order

tools.name: crypto_post-trading-order
tools.description: Places a new crypto trading order with an order type. **Note**: Depending on the type used in the request body, you must include the respective order configuration in the request body. For order configurations that support both `asset_quantity` or `quote_amount`, only one can be present in the request body. Required: client_order_id, side, symbol, type. Optional: limit_order_config, market_order_config, stop_limit_order_config (plus 1 more). Returns the new OrderResponse.

params.name: client_order_id
params.description: User input order id for idempotency validation

params.name: limit-order-config-asset-quantity
params.description: The quantity of base currency bought or sold

params.name: limit-order-config-limit-price
params.description: The limit price of the order bought or sold

params.name: limit-order-config-quote-amount
params.description: The amount of quote currency bought or sold

params.name: market-order-config-asset-quantity
params.description: The quantity of base currency bought or sold

params.name: side
params.description: Buy or sell

params.name: stop-limit-order-config-asset-quantity
params.description: The quantity of base currency bought or sold

params.name: stop-limit-order-config-limit-price
params.description: The limit price of the order bought or sold

params.name: stop-limit-order-config-quote-amount
params.description: The amount of quote currency bought or sold

params.name: stop-limit-order-config-stop-price
params.description: The stop price of the order bought or sold

params.name: stop-limit-order-config-time-in-force
params.description: The time in force of the order. - **Good til canceled (gtc):** The order remains open until it's filled or the user cancels it. - **Good for day (gfd):** The order remains open until the end of the day or when the user cancels it. - **Good for week (gfw):** The order remains open until the end of the week or when the user cancels it. - **Good for month (gfm):** The order remains open until the end of the month or when the user cancels it.

params.name: stop-loss-order-config-asset-quantity
params.description: The quantity of base currency bought or sold

params.name: stop-loss-order-config-quote-amount
params.description: The amount of quote currency bought or sold

params.name: stop-loss-order-config-stop-price
params.description: The stop price of the order bought or sold

params.name: stop-loss-order-config-time-in-force
params.description: The time in force of the order. - **Good til canceled (gtc):** The order remains open until it's filled or the user cancels it. - **Good for day (gfd):** The order remains open until the end of the day or when the user cancels it. - **Good for week (gfw):** The order remains open until the end of the week or when the user cancels it. - **Good for month (gfm):** The order remains open until the end of the month or when the user cancels it.

params.name: symbol
params.description: Example: **BTC-USD** Currency pair symbol. Ensure that the symbol is provided in all uppercase.

params.name: type
params.description: Type of order

tools.name: crypto_trading-account-details
tools.description: Fetches the Robinhood Crypto account details for the current user. Returns the Account.

tools.name: crypto_trading-accounts
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of crypto trading accounts for the authenticated user. Returns account details including buying power, status, and fee tier information. Optional: cursor, limit.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Optional cursor for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.description: Optional page size limit. Specifies the number of accounts to return per page. Maximum value is determined by system configuration.

tools.name: crypto_trading-cancel-order
tools.description: Cancels an open crypto trading order. Required: id. Returns the new V2CryptoOrder.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the order to cancel.

tools.name: crypto_trading-holdings
tools.description: Fetch a list of holdings for the current user. Optional: asset_code, limit, cursor. Returns the CryptoTradingHoldingsResponse.

params.name: asset_code
params.description: The asset code(s) to retrieve data for. Multiple asset codes can be provided by using the 'asset_code' parameter with different values. Ensure that the asset codes are provided in all uppercase. For example, **?asset_code=BTC&asset_code=ETH**

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of results per page size

params.name: cursor
params.description: Cursor

tools.name: crypto_trading-holdings-trading
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of crypto holdings for a specific account. Returns the total quantity and available quantity for each asset held in the account. The `account_number` query parameter is required. Required: account_number. Optional: asset_code, cursor, limit.

params.name: account_number
params.description: **Required.** The account number to retrieve holdings for. This parameter must be provided.

params.name: asset_code
params.description: Optional. Filter holdings by specific asset code (e.g., 'BTC').

params.name: cursor
params.description: Optional cursor for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.description: Optional page size limit. Specifies the number of results to return per page. Maximum value is determined by system configuration.

tools.name: crypto_trading-orders
tools.description: Fetch a list of orders for the current user. Optional: created_at_start, created_at_end, symbol (plus 8 more). Returns the CryptoTradingOrdersResponse.

params.name: created_at_start
params.description: Filter by created at start time range (greater than or equal to) in ISO 8601 format

params.name: created_at_end
params.description: Filter by created at end time range (less than or equal to) in ISO 8601 format

params.name: symbol
params.description: Currency pair symbol. Ensure that the symbol is provided in all uppercase.

params.name: id
params.description: Order ID, unique per order

params.name: side
params.description: Buy or sell

params.name: state
params.description: State of the order

params.name: type
params.description: Type of order

params.name: updated_at_start
params.description: Filter by updated at start time range (greater than or equal to) in ISO 8601 format

params.name: updated_at_end
params.description: Filter by updated at end time range (less than or equal to) in ISO 8601 format

params.name: cursor
params.description: Cursor

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of results per page size

tools.name: crypto_trading-orders-get
tools.description: Fetch a list of orders for the current user in a specific account. Required: account_number. Optional: cursor, created_at_start, created_at_end (plus 6 more). Returns the V2OrdersResponse.

params.name: account_number
params.description: Required. The number of the crypto account to retrieve orders from.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Optional cursor for pagination.

params.name: created_at_start
params.description: Filter orders created on or after this timestamp (ISO 8601 format).

params.name: created_at_end
params.description: Filter orders created on or before this timestamp (ISO 8601 format).

params.name: updated_at_start
params.description: Filter orders updated on or after this timestamp (ISO 8601 format).

params.name: updated_at_end
params.description: Filter orders updated on or before this timestamp (ISO 8601 format).

params.name: symbol
params.description: Filter orders by trading pair symbol.

params.name: side
params.description: Filter orders by side.

params.name: type
params.description: Filter orders by type.

params.name: state
params.description: Filter orders by state.

tools.name: crypto_trading-orders-post
tools.description: Place a new crypto trading order with an order type. This endpoint only supports placing orders on USD symbols that have `is_api_tradable=true` as defined in our Get Crypto Trading Pairs endpoint. **Note:** Depending on the type used in the request body, you must include the respective order configuration in the request body. For order configurations that support both `asset_quantity` or `quote_amount`, only one can be present in the request body. Required: account_number, client_order_id, side, symbol, type. Optional: limit_order_config, market_order_config, stop_limit_order_config (plus 1 more). Returns the new V2CryptoOrder.

params.description: Required. The number of the crypto account to place orders from.

params.description: The quantity of base currency bought or sold

params.description: The time in force of the order. - **Good til canceled (gtc):** The order remains open until it's filled or the user cancels it. - **Good for day (gfd):** The order remains open until the end of the day or when the user cancels it. - **Good for week (gfw):** The order remains open until the end of the week or when the user cancels it. - **Good for month (gfm):** The order remains open until the end of the month or when the user cancels it.

params.description: The time in force of the order. - **Good til canceled (gtc):** The order remains open until it's filled or the user cancels it. - **Good for day (gfd):** The order remains open until the end of the day or when the user cancels it. - **Good for week (gfw):** The order remains open until the end of the week or when the user cancels it. - **Good for month (gfm):** The order remains open until the end of the month or when the user cancels it.

tools.name: crypto_trading-trading-pairs
tools.description: Fetch a list of trading pairs. Optional: symbol, limit, cursor. Returns the CryptoTradingTradingPairsResponse.

params.name: symbol
params.description: List of trading pair symbol(s) to retrieve data for. Multiple symbols can be provided by using the 'symbol' parameter with different values. Ensure that the symbol(s) are provided in all uppercase. For example, **?symbol=BTC-USD&symbol=ETH-USD**

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of results per page size

params.name: cursor
params.description: Cursor

tools.name: crypto_trading-trading-pairs-trading
tools.description: Fetch a paginated list of available trading pairs for crypto trading. Returns trading pair details including price increments, order size limits, and tradability status. Trading pairs can be filtered by symbol. Optional: symbol, cursor, limit.

params.name: symbol
params.description: Optional. Filter by trading pair symbol(s). Example: `symbol=BTC-USD`. Multiple symbols can be provided by using the 'symbol' parameter with different values. Ensure that the symbol(s) are provided in all uppercase. For example, `?symbol=BTC-USD&symbol=ETH-USD`. If the 'symbol' parameter is not provided, this endpoint will return all tradable currency pairs.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Optional cursor for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.description: Optional page size limit. Specifies the number of results to return per page. Maximum value is determined by system configuration.
